message:
test(patterns): pin EMAIL_ADDRESS regex to ASCII letter classes

The EMAIL_ADDRESS detection pattern is one of the core redaction
rules used by the pages pipeline. It is important that it keeps
using explicit ASCII \`[A-Za-z]\` character classes rather than
\\p Unicode property classes: the pages pipeline ships regexes via
the \`regex\` crate and we want deterministic, auditable matching
against addresses that use the ASCII-only local part and domain-
label rules that mail-transfer-agents actually accept. Unicode
class drift would silently change what we redact (and what we
don't).

Add \`test_email_pattern_uses_ascii_letter_classes\`:

- Positive: matches a realistic ASCII address with mixed case and
  underscores (\"Contact USER_123@example.COM\") — locks in that
  the regex still picks up the common forms.
- Negative: rejects an address whose TLD label uses a non-ASCII
  script (\"user@example.δοκιμή\"). If someone widens the class
  to \\p{L}, this assertion fails.
- Structural: asserts the pattern SOURCE contains \`[A-Za-z]\`
  and does NOT contain \`\\p\`. Catches regressions that keep the
  behavior by accident while introducing \\p to the source (and
  would therefore drift under newer regex-crate Unicode versions).

Also applies rustfmt's preferred single-expression wrapping to
the adjacent \`test_patterns_by_category\` assertion block (no
logic change) so the file is rustfmt-clean.

Pure test + format change.
